methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deepnight is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Lucas Young and Thomas Li, having raised $5.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 5 same-sector companies at Seed and Series A stages, supplemented by a broader universe of 10 comparable private firms and 2 public defense-tech names.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised ($6M) as a value proxy and applying a standard 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier, implies a valuation of $14M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparable companies yields a $150M implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $60M (Tenna Systems at Seed) to $500M (Stark Defence at Series A). The disparity reflects both stage variation and company-specific performance within the defense sector.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $12M to $172M, with a median of $82M. The wide spread reflects uncertainty around Deepnight's competitive position relative to later-stage peers and whether growth trajectory will track closer to the Seed-stage ($60M) or Series A median ($300M+) comparables. Key near-term valuation drivers include technology differentiation, customer traction, and fundraising momentum toward Series A. The peer-median method carries higher weight given stage heterogeneity, suggesting a central estimate in the $75M–$95M range, contingent on validation of defensibility claims within the crowded defense-tech landscape.
